behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit, because the failure is in deterministic lint logic over template text. Re-check against higher levels: Integration/System/Agentic are not required since behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check" and does not require harness assembly, OS/network/subprocess, or model output. Disqualifier for higher levels: "static" and "lint check".
behaviors.test_description: Feed template strings into the lint function, including one containing a verbatim phase instruction copied from SKILL.md, and assert it is rejected with the expected rule violation while non-leaking templates pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration because leakage can be introduced only after module handoff (template + interpolation + fixture expansion). Re-check: Unit is too low because single-function checks miss cross-module assembly defects; System not needed because no real OS/network/subprocess boundary is required beyond local in-process harness wiring; Agentic/Workflow not needed because this is prompt-content validation, not model judgment. Disqualifier for Agentic: behavior text focuses on "fully assembled prompt ... contains no leakage".
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction path with production-like fixtures/interpolation and assert the final prompt text does not contain banned phase-instruction fragments from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration since the failure boundary is module interaction between file-backed skill loader, prompt/test invocation, and validation wiring. Re-check: Unit is too low because it would not prove context comes from real plugin/skill files. Agentic considered but rejected: behavior text is about source-of-truth wiring ("loads skill context from plugin/skill files") rather than model-output quality. System not required unless real OS-specific file event/process behavior is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the test invocation against real plugin/skill file inputs while keeping prompt rules unchanged, and assert the harness reports that behavior evidence derives from loaded skill context (not embedded phase rules).

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration because this verifies harness configurability and file-variant injection across modules. Re-check: Unit is too low since a single function test cannot prove the full harness run path accepts variant skill files with unchanged prompt text. System rejected because behavior does not require real subprocess/network/OS edge conditions; Agentic rejected because this behavior is run-capability, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness invocations (baseline skill and degraded/missing SKILL.md variant) with byte-identical user prompt text and assert both runs execute, with only skill-file input differing.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Agentic because pass/fail depends on how the real model responds to changed skill context under identical prompt text. Re-check: Integration with stubs can verify wiring but cannot validate that skill-content regression changes actual model behavior; that is the deterministic-assertion trap. Workflow considered but rejected because behavior describes a single prompt-response decision, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same user-style prompt against baseline and degraded SKILL.md using the live model; assert baseline satisfies behavioral constraints and degraded variant fails those same constraints.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration since the failure is ordering across harness components (model response handling, artifact writer, parser). Re-check: Unit may miss real sequencing bugs across module boundaries; System rejected because local artifact persistence can be validated without subprocess/network dependencies; Agentic rejected because model output content quality is irrelevant to the ordering guarantee.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument the harness pipeline with a deterministic model stub response, execute one run, and assert an unmodified raw-output artifact exists before parser/extractor hooks are invoked.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit because this is assertion logic correctness over provided outputs. Re-check: Integration not required unless validating harness plumbing; here the behavior is specifically about assertion semantics. Agentic/System/Workflow rejected because no real model, network, subprocess, or multi-step pipeline is required to expose keyword-vs-structure false positives.
behaviors.test_description: Provide crafted outputs where keywords are present but fields are misplaced or schema paths are wrong, and assert structural validators fail while keyword-only checks would have passed.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Agentic because behavior explicitly requires "live model execution" and depends on real model-produced variation where keyword presence can mask structural defects. Re-check: Integration with fixtures would hide these real regressions; Workflow considered but rejected because text does not require multiple agentic steps, only one live execution evaluated by stronger assertions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the behavioral smoke test against the live model and compare outcomes of structural assertions versus keyword-only checks, asserting at least one regression case is caught only by structural constraints.
